title: BlackRock (BLK)Intrinsic Value
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on BlackRock using FY2025 actuals. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what BLK is really worth.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-pni5cwlx>
A 9% growth rate assumes BlackRock continues to capture net inflows in ETFs (iShares) and its Aladdin technology platform. While base fees face pressure, volume and tech revenue should sustain high single-digit bottom-line growth.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-pni5cwlx>
10Y Treasury: 4.18%. For a diversified, dominant asset manager, a 9.5% discount rate provides a solid hurdle rate, slightly below riskier tech names but above the risk-free rate.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-pni5cwlx>
3.0% roughly matches nominal global GDP growth. An asset manager's long-term growth is inextricably linked to global wealth creation and market appreciation.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use DCF instead of a P/E or EV/EBITDA multiple?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "BlackRock (BLK) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, March 18, 2026.
